dramatic_function: Marriage injury shared property and attempted recovery
power_asymmetry: Kōvalaṉ depends on Kaṇṇaki's property and endurance
interpretation_limit: Her cooperation does not erase abandonment

dramatic_function: Economic access becomes shared artistic relationship
power_asymmetry: He can purchase access but not control her meaning
interpretation_limit: Do not reduce the bond to seduction

dramatic_function: Her artistic answer triggers his interpretive break
power_asymmetry: His suspicion dominates the immediate outcome
interpretation_limit: Suspicion is not proof of infidelity

dramatic_function: The flower-letter moves private appeal through a trusted messenger
power_asymmetry: Messenger controls access but not meaning
interpretation_limit: Do not turn the letter into generic romantic correspondence

dramatic_function: Kauṇṭi makes terrain and bodily vulnerability morally visible
power_asymmetry: Kauṇṭi has route authority while Kaṇṇaki bears the bodily cost
interpretation_limit: Protection is not supernatural control

dramatic_function: Ascetic protection is transferred to pastoral household protection
power_asymmetry: Kauṇṭi assesses need and Mādhari controls local shelter
interpretation_limit: Do not reduce the handover to sentiment

dramatic_function: Food shelter and women's care restore temporary safety
power_asymmetry: Mādhari has local household power over an exposed traveller
interpretation_limit: Do not idealise refuge as permanent security

dramatic_function: Dēvanti offers a ritual route and Kaṇṇaki refuses it
power_asymmetry: Dēvanti has ritual confidence but Kaṇṇaki retains judgement
interpretation_limit: Direct Canto 9 anchor must accompany this contextual HWT link

dramatic_function: Possessed speech names exceptional status before civic proof
power_asymmetry: Ritual community interprets while Kaṇṇaki remains a traveller
interpretation_limit: Recognition must not replace later legal evidence

dramatic_function: Craft expertise recasts a trader as a thief
power_asymmetry: The goldsmith controls technical credibility and local access
interpretation_limit: Do not make deception implausibly theatrical

dramatic_function: Specialised assertion enters royal decision without testing
power_asymmetry: The king has coercive power but relies on unverified expertise
interpretation_limit: Individual deceit does not excuse sovereign haste

dramatic_function: Royal authority converts accusation into irreversible killing
power_asymmetry: Kōvalaṉ has no effective procedural protection
interpretation_limit: Execution must not appear deserved by fate

dramatic_function: Material proof reverses the court's classification
power_asymmetry: She lacks rank but possesses decisive knowledge and object
interpretation_limit: Her power must not depend only on rage

dramatic_function: The queen moves from omen-bearing consort to witness of irreversible grief
power_asymmetry: Royal status cannot repair Kaṇṇaki's loss
interpretation_limit: Do not invent jealousy or rivalry

dramatic_function: The city deity widens the causal frame after the human case is proven
power_asymmetry: Divine authority explains but arrives after institutional failure
interpretation_limit: Karma must not absolve government

dramatic_function: Citizens interpret the bent sceptre and guide her to the body
power_asymmetry: Crowd knowledge gives visibility but not immediate remedy
interpretation_limit: Do not make the city uniformly passive or hostile

dramatic_function: Hill witnesses report Kaṇṇaki's final appearance in Cēra country
power_asymmetry: Local knowledge precedes royal interpretation
interpretation_limit: Do not let the court claim original discovery

dramatic_function: Dispersed events become a political account for the Cēra ruler
power_asymmetry: Narrative knowledge influences sovereign response
interpretation_limit: Do not merge Sāttanār with an omniscient author

dramatic_function: The queen recommends Pattini honour and gives ethical direction
power_asymmetry: She advises within royal hierarchy but changes policy
interpretation_limit: Do not reduce her to ceremonial assent

dramatic_function: Reports of deaths renunciations and grief interrupt martial triumph
power_asymmetry: The king controls force while Māṭalan controls dispersed knowledge
interpretation_limit: Exposition must alter judgement and policy

dramatic_function: Royal institutions turn testimony into stone shrine festival and recurring worship
power_asymmetry: The state has scale but does not originate her truth
interpretation_limit: Do not replace Kaṇṇaki with the king as moral protagonist

dramatic_function: A companion carries earlier life grief and divine speech into public worship
power_asymmetry: Institutional ritual can frame personal memory
interpretation_limit: Do not erase Dēvanti's prior relationship to Kaṇṇaki

dramatic_function: Royal command becomes built form through ritual calendrical and craft labour
power_asymmetry: The king commissions while specialists control technical execution
interpretation_limit: Do not depict shrine creation as a single heroic act

dramatic_function: Her claim expands from husband and king to the city's moral community
power_asymmetry: Her command gains scale after public proof
interpretation_limit: Selective fire and protected groups must remain

dramatic_function: Separate deaths griefs and renunciations are joined into one causal memory network
power_asymmetry: He transmits stories of people who cannot speak in the scene
interpretation_limit: Do not let his account erase their distinct experiences
